gazette: General Directorate of Trade Research and Risk Assessment
title: Georgia imposes accredited analysis requirement on non-animal food products
bodyHtml:
Georgia has notified the World Trade Organization that as of June 1, 2023, accredited analysis is required for non-animal food products imported from . various countries, including Türkiye.  For the consignments from Türkiye, limited products such as dried figs and fig products, pistachios and pistachio kernels, grape leaves, carob, keratin, lemon, grapefruit and citrus products, pomegranates, sweet peppers, apricot kernels, and thyme are subject to analysis for aflatoxin, pesticide residues, cyanide in apricot kernels, and alkaloids in thyme.

Below are the relevant notification to the WTO Health-Plant Health Committee and the links to the related legislation:

-WTO Notification

-Related Legislation

The details of which products (with HS codes) are subject to which analysis (aflatoxin, pesticide, etc.) are included in the annex of the legislation, categorized by countries.

Click here for the relevant announcement.
